nodes.bodyMarkdown:
---
name: rapid-update-writer skill
description: New writing skill created for fast-paced AI/tech update scripts — blends Vaibhav Sisinty's structure with Nikunj's voice
type: project
---

Created `rapid-update-writer` skill at `Projects/_templates/skills/rapid-update-writer/SKILL.md`.

Blends Vaibhav Sisinty's scriptwriting structure (170 WPM, numbered updates, zero transitions, front-loaded hooks, story hooks mid-video) with Nikunj's voice (warmth, humility, ellipsis pivots, contradiction-naming, soft landings, no exclamation marks).

**Why:** Nikunj needs a distinct writing mode for AI update videos (faceless channel, Neither Bank) that's punchier and faster than writer-skill-v2 but still sounds like him, not like generic tech YouTube.

**How to apply:** Invoke `rapid-update-writer` for any weekly AI update script, tech news roundup, or "X things that happened" format. Use writer-skill-v2 for long-form essays, personal reflection, philosophical pieces. The two skills share voice rules but differ in pace, sentence length defaults, and structure.

Source analysis: Vaibhav Sisinty's YouTube channel (403K subs, @VaibhavSisinty) — transcript analysis of 3 videos confirmed 164-178 WPM, ~6.5 words/segment, near-zero pauses, English-only delivery.

name: YouTube Research Pipeline (YtBddyBot)
description: Telegram bot + n8n + NotebookLM bridge. Fully operational with polling, timeouts, error handling. 39 nodes, 2 workflows.
type: project
originSessionId: e3b33936-a9c8-4fe4-853f-756993e63816
---
## Current State (2026-03-20) — FULLY OPERATIONAL

**Bot**: @YtBddyBot — credential ID `VGsxOOmLBSCjiXtl`
**Main Workflow**: `XQh4D9taQbJcWYxB` — 39 nodes, active
**Keep-Alive Workflow**: `u8ZvJoZ2yIC1CYvH` — 4 nodes, active, runs every 4 hours
**Bridge**: Docker container `notebooklm-bridge` on port 8100
**Project dir**: `[redacted]\Projects\automation-projects\research-pipeline\`
**Surya's Telegram chat ID**: `6272541552`

## n8n Instance
- URL: http://localhost:5678
- Community edition (no env variables feature — API keys stay in workflow JSON)
- Login: `[redacted]` / `Nikunj911@`
- Only 2 active workflows (old duplicates deleted)
- webhookId must be `yt-research-tg` on Telegram Trigger

## Bot Commands
- `/start`, `/new`, `/reset` → reset session, show welcome
- `/list`, `/notebooks` → show all NotebookLM notebooks
- `/help`, `/commands` → show available commands
- `/types`, `/aliases`, `/content` → show all content types with aliases
- Content types: podcast, audio, report, blog, summary, slides, ppt, video, cinematic, mind map, infographic, pic, image, quiz, flashcards, table
- Any other text (3+ chars) → YouTube search → notebook creation
- Unknown `/commands` or <3 chars → "didn't recognize" message

## Architecture
Telegram → n8n (39 nodes) → FastAPI bridge (port 8100) → NotebookLM CLI

### Non-blocking generation with polling
- `POST /generate/start` → returns immediately with artifact_id
- `GET /generate/status/{chat_id}` → lock-free status polling
- n8n polls every 30s, sends soft timeout msg once, hard timeout to end
- Max 100 poll iterations safety cap (~50 min)

### Per-content-type timeouts
| Type | Soft | Hard |
|------|------|------|
| Mind map | 10s | 30s |
| Report, infographic, quiz, flashcards, slides, table | 10 min | 20 min |
| Audio/podcast | 15 min | 25 min |
| Video, cinematic | 25 min | 50 min |

## Error Handling
- `onError: continueRegardless` on YouTube Search, Create Notebook, Add Sources, Start Generation
- Zero YouTube results → "No videos found" message
- Generate before notebook → error message to user
- Auth expired → error message to user
- Session persistence to `downloads/sessions.json` (survives bridge restarts)

## Bridge Endpoints
| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| GET | `/health` | Health check |
| GET | `/auth/check` | Auth check + keep-alive |
| GET | `/notebooks/list` | List all notebooks |
| POST | `/notebook/create` | Create new notebook |
| POST | `/notebook/select` | Select existing notebook |
| POST | `/source/add-batch` | Add YouTube URLs as sources |
| POST | `/generate/start` | Start generation (non-blocking) |
| GET | `/generate/status/{chat_id}` | Poll generation status (lock-free) |
| POST | `/generate` | Generate blocking (backward compat) |
| GET | `/download/{chat_id}/{type}` | Download generated artifact |

## Known Limitations
- n8n Community: no env variables, API key hardcoded in workflow
- Cloudflare tunnel URL changes on container recreation
- No notebook selection from /list (just shows list)
- NotebookLM auth expires — keep-alive n8n workflow (every 4h) mitigates, re-auth: `notebooklm login`

## 2026-04-19 cleanup
- Deleted two broken Windows scheduled tasks: `NotebookLMAuthRefresh` and `NotebookLM_Auth_Refresh`
- Both fired daily 7am + 2min post-logon + intra-day repeat, pointing to `bridge/refresh_auth.py` which never existed (silently failing with LastTaskResult 2)
- Auth refresh now relies solely on the n8n keep-alive workflow hitting `/auth/check` every 4h
- If auth starts expiring again, rebuild a real `refresh_auth.py` before re-registering any scheduled task

nodes.label: project notebooklm content pipeline
nodes.description: NotebookLM → Content Pipeline — 50 YT videos → report → Nikunj rewrite → ElevenLabs → official B-roll video. Test clip done. Next: full 10-min video
nodes.bodyMarkdown:
---
name: NotebookLM → Content Pipeline (AI Updates)
description: End-to-end pipeline from NotebookLM reports to narrated video with official B-roll. Proven with test clip. Ready to scale to full production.
type: project
---

## What Was Built (2026-03-20)

A complete content pipeline: NotebookLM report (50 YouTube sources) → rewrite in Nikunj's voice → ElevenLabs narration → official source B-roll → assembled video.

## Pipeline Steps (all working)

1. **NotebookLM report generation** — Feed 50 YouTube videos, generate blog-post report
2. **Voice rewrite** — Rewrite report in two styles:
   - **writer-skill-v2** (Matt Shumer + Nikunj) — long-form reflective essay
   - **rapid-update-writer** (Vaibhav structure + Nikunj voice) — fast-paced numbered updates
3. **ElevenLabs narration** — Generate audio with Nikunj Voice clone
4. **Official source B-roll** — Screenshots (browser MCP) + video clips (yt-dlp) from real sources
5. **Video assembly** — ffmpeg combines B-roll with narration audio

## Key Decisions

- **Rapid-update style was revised**: Original V2 sounded too much like Vaibhav Sisinty's channel. Fixed by keeping Vaibhav's *structure* (numbered updates, density) but using Nikunj's *natural voice* (flowing sentences, ellipsis pivots, warmth). The V1 essay voice and V2 script voice now sound like the same person in different gears.
- **Official B-roll, NOT Pexels**: Screenshots from actual sources (Anthropic blog, Moltbook, tweets, CNBC) + video clips from YouTube demos. Makes content credible and visuals match narration.
- **B-roll naming convention**: Files named by script order (`01-opening-moltbook-homepage.png`, `04-update1-claude-code-demo.mp4`) so assembly order is automatic.
- **Source attribution**: Add small credit text on video clips for fair use compliance.
- **Daniel voice** (ElevenLabs premade, British broadcaster) recommended as alternative voice — steady, warm, good contrast with Nikunj clone.

## Audio Settings Used

- **Essay style**: `eleven_multilingual_v2`, stability 0.40, similarity 0.80, style 0.45, speed default, speaker boost ON
- **Script style**: Same multilingual model (NOT turbo — turbo was too fast/punchy for revised Nikunj voice)
- Always normalize to -16 LUFS: `ffmpeg -y -i input.mp3 -af "loudnorm=I=-16:TP=-1.5:LRA=11" output-LOUD.mp3`

## Files Created

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `Projects/_templates/ai-updates-essay-v1.md` | Full essay (~2,500 words, writer-skill-v2) |
| `Projects/_templates/ai-updates-script-v2.md` | Full script (~1,700 words, revised rapid-update) |
| `Projects/faceless-ai-channel/audio-tests/essay-v1-nikunj-LOUD.mp3` | Essay excerpt, Nikunj voice |
| `Projects/faceless-ai-channel/audio-tests/essay-v1-daniel-LOUD.mp3` | Essay excerpt, Daniel voice |
| `Projects/faceless-ai-channel/audio-tests/script-v2-revised-nikunj-LOUD.mp3` | Script excerpt, Nikunj voice (revised) |
| `Projects/faceless-ai-channel/audio-tests/test-clip-nikunj-LOUD.mp3` | Extended test clip audio (opening + updates 1-2) |
| `Projects/faceless-ai-channel/audio-tests/test-clip-v2.mp4` | Assembled test video with official B-roll (screenshots + video clips) |
| `Projects/faceless-ai-channel/audio-tests/broll-screenshots/` | 8 named screenshots matching script order |
| `Projects/faceless-ai-channel/audio-tests/broll-videos/` | Claude Code demo + Alex Finn phone call video clips |

## NotebookLM Notebook

- **Name**: "Ai updates"
- **ID**: `47482162-e269-45b6-8881-69b95be5e814`
- **Sources**: 50 YouTube videos (all ready)
- **Report artifact**: `24088b67-753c-4b53-8c21-a6e553ea6193` (completed)

## Legal Position

Using short clips (10-30s) with commentary = fair use. Official company demos are published for publicity. Always credit sources visually. Same approach as MKBHD, Fireship, etc. Can automate source attribution overlay.

## Next Steps

- Scale to full 10-minute video (all 7 updates)
- Research official sources for updates 3-7 (Google Autobrowse, GPT 5.3, Dario Amodei, Google Stitch, cyberattack)
- Download video clips for remaining updates
- Automate source attribution overlay on clips
- Build Remotion template for production-quality rendering (vs. current ffmpeg slideshow)
- Consider word-level captions (Whisper) for engagement
- Explore making this a repeatable weekly pipeline: NotebookLM → rewrite → narrate → B-roll → render

nodes.label: paperclip
nodes.bodyMarkdown:
Quickstart · Docs · GitHub · Discord



## What is Paperclip? # Open-source orchestration for zero-human companies **If OpenClaw is an _employee_, Paperclip is the _company_** Paperclip is a Node.js server and React UI that orchestrates a team of AI agents to run a business. Bring your own agents, assign goals, and track your agents' work and costs from one dashboard. It looks like a task manager — but under the hood it has org charts, budgets, governance, goal alignment, and agent coordination. **Manage business goals, not pull requests.** | | Step | Example | | ------ | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | | **01** | Define the goal | _"Build the #1 AI note-taking app to $1M MRR."_ | | **02** | Hire the team | CEO, CTO, engineers, designers, marketers — any bot, any provider. | | **03** | Approve and run | Review strategy. Set budgets. Hit go. Monitor from the dashboard. |
> **COMING SOON: Clipmart** — Download and run entire companies with one click. Browse pre-built company templates — full org structures, agent configs, and skills — and import them into your Paperclip instance in seconds.
Works
with
OpenClaw
Claude Code
Codex
Cursor
Bash
HTTP
If it can receive a heartbeat, it's hired.

## Paperclip is right for you if - ✅ You want to build **autonomous AI companies** - ✅ You **coordinate many different agents** (OpenClaw, Codex, Claude, Cursor) toward a common goal - ✅ You have **20 simultaneous Claude Code terminals** open and lose track of what everyone is doing - ✅ You want agents running **autonomously 24/7**, but still want to audit work and chime in when needed - ✅ You want to **monitor costs** and enforce budgets - ✅ You want a process for managing agents that **feels like using a task manager** - ✅ You want to manage your autonomous businesses **from your phone**
## Features

🔌 Bring Your Own Agent

Any agent, any runtime, one org chart. If it can receive a heartbeat, it's hired.

🎯 Goal Alignment

Every task traces back to the company mission. Agents know what to do and why.

💓 Heartbeats

Agents wake on a schedule, check work, and act. Delegation flows up and down the org chart.

💰 Cost Control

Monthly budgets per agent. When they hit the limit, they stop. No runaway costs.

🏢 Multi-Company

One deployment, many companies. Complete data isolation. One control plane for your portfolio.

🎫 Ticket System

Every conversation traced. Every decision explained. Full tool-call tracing and immutable audit log.

🛡️ Governance

You're the board. Approve hires, override strategy, pause or terminate any agent — at any time.

📊 Org Chart

Hierarchies, roles, reporting lines. Your agents have a boss, a title, and a job description.

📱 Mobile Ready

Monitor and manage your autonomous businesses from anywhere.

## Problems Paperclip solves | Without Paperclip | With Paperclip | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | ❌ You have 20 Claude Code tabs open and can't track which one does what. On reboot you lose everything. | ✅ Tasks are ticket-based, conversations are threaded, sessions persist across reboots. | | ❌ You manually gather context from several places to remind your bot what you're actually doing. | ✅ Context flows from the task up through the project and company goals — your agent always knows what to do and why. | | ❌ Folders of agent configs are disorganized and you're re-inventing task management, communication, and coordination between agents. | ✅ Paperclip gives you org charts, ticketing, delegation, and governance out of the box — so you run a company, not a pile of scripts. | | ❌ Runaway loops waste hundreds of dollars of tokens and max your quota before you even know what happened. | ✅ Cost tracking surfaces token budgets and throttles agents when they're out. Management prioritizes with budgets. | | ❌ You have recurring jobs (customer support, social, reports) and have to remember to manually kick them off. | ✅ Heartbeats handle regular work on a schedule. Management supervises. | | ❌ You have an idea, you have to find your repo, fire up Claude Code, keep a tab open, and babysit it. | ✅ Add a task in Paperclip. Your coding agent works on it until it's done. Management reviews their work. |
## Why Paperclip is special Paperclip handles the hard orchestration details correctly. | | | | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Atomic execution.** | Task checkout and budget enforcement are atomic, so no double-work and no runaway spend. | | **Persistent agent state.** | Agents resume the same task context across heartbeats instead of restarting from scratch. | | **Runtime skill injection.** | Agents can learn Paperclip workflows and project context at runtime, without retraining. | | **Governance with rollback.** | Approval gates are enforced, config changes are revisioned, and bad changes can be rolled back safely. | | **Goal-aware execution.** | Tasks carry full goal ancestry so agents consistently see the "why," not just a title. | | **Portable company templates.** | Export/import orgs, agents, and skills with secret scrubbing and collision handling. | | **True multi-company isolation.** | Every entity is company-scoped, so one deployment can run many companies with separate data and audit trails. |
## What Paperclip is not | | | | ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Not a chatbot.** | Agents have jobs, not chat windows. | | **Not an agent framework.** | We don't tell you how to build agents. We tell you how to run a company made of them. | | **Not a workflow builder.** | No drag-and-drop pipelines. Paperclip models companies — with org charts, goals, budgets, and governance. | | **Not a prompt manager.** | Agents bring their own prompts, models, and runtimes. Paperclip manages the organization they work in. | | **Not a single-agent tool.** | This is for teams. If you have one agent, you probably don't need Paperclip. If you have twenty — you definitely do. | | **Not a code review tool.** | Paperclip orchestrates work, not pull requests. Bring your own review process. |
## Quickstart Open source. Self-hosted. No Paperclip account required. ```bash npx paperclipai onboard --yes ``` If you already have Paperclip configured, rerunning `onboard` keeps the existing config in place. Use `paperclipai configure` to edit settings. Or manually: ```bash git clone https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip.git cd paperclip pnpm install pnpm dev ``` This starts the API server at `http://localhost:3100`. An embedded PostgreSQL database is created automatically — no setup required. > **Requirements:** Node.js 20+, pnpm 9.15+
## FAQ **What does a typical setup look like?** Locally, a single Node.js process manages an embedded Postgres and local file storage. For production, point it at your own Postgres and deploy however you like. Configure projects, agents, and goals — the agents take care of the rest. If you're a solo-entreprenuer you can use Tailscale to access Paperclip on the go. Then later you can deploy to e.g. Vercel when you need it. **Can I run multiple companies?** Yes. A single deployment can run an unlimited number of companies with complete data isolation. **How is Paperclip different from agents like OpenClaw or Claude Code?** Paperclip _uses_ those agents. It orchestrates them into a company — with org charts, budgets, goals, governance, and accountability. **Why should I use Paperclip instead of just pointing my OpenClaw to Asana or Trello?** Agent orchestration has subtleties in how you coordinate who has work checked out, how to maintain sessions, monitoring costs, establishing governance - Paperclip does this for you. (Bring-your-own-ticket-system is on the Roadmap) **Do agents run continuously?** By default, agents run on scheduled heartbeats and event-based triggers (task assignment, @-mentions). You can also hook in continuous agents like OpenClaw. You bring your agent and Paperclip coordinates.
## Development ```bash pnpm dev # Full dev (API + UI, watch mode) pnpm dev:once # Full dev without file watching pnpm dev:server # Server only pnpm build # Build all pnpm typecheck # Type checking pnpm test:run # Run tests pnpm db:generate # Generate DB migration pnpm db:migrate # Apply migrations ``` See [doc/DEVELOPING.md](doc/DEVELOPING.md) for the full development guide.
## Roadmap - ✅ Plugin system (e.g. add a knowledge base, custom tracing, queues, etc) - ✅ Get OpenClaw / claw-style agent employees - ✅ companies.sh - import and export entire organizations - ✅ Easy AGENTS.md configurations - ✅ Skills Manager - ✅ Scheduled Routines - ✅ Better Budgeting - ⚪ Artifacts & Deployments - ⚪ CEO Chat - ⚪ MAXIMIZER MODE - ⚪ Multiple Human Users - ⚪ Cloud / Sandbox agents (e.g. Cursor / e2b agents) - ⚪ Cloud deployments - ⚪ Desktop App

nodes.bodyMarkdown:
---
name: Nifty50 Trading System
description: ORB + PDH/PDL strategies PASS all thresholds (Sharpe 1.6+, PF 1.3+, DD <2%). First positive result. Ready for paper trading.
type: project
originSessionId: 8627d90b-1b8c-4086-a53e-1b285a6dbdf3
---
## Current Status (2026-04-16)

**Three strategy iterations completed. ORB and PDH/PDL are paper-trade ready.**

### Iteration 1: Martingale (DEAD)
- Supertrend 1-min, progressive sizing 1→3→5→7
- Result: -₹24.5L over 2 years, Sharpe -2.89
- Cause: costs amplified by martingale faster than wins

### Iteration 2: Trend Continuation (DEAD)
- Supertrend on 5/10/15/30-min, fixed 1-lot
- 192 configs tested, best: Sharpe 0.804, PF 1.109
- Barely above threshold, fragile edge, 84% max DD

### Iteration 3: ORB + PDH/PDL (PASS — 2026-04-16)

**ORB Best: 30-min range, trail 30pt, 5pt buffer, EMA10 filter**
- Sharpe 1.61, PF 1.42, 283 trades, max DD -1.56%
- Split-sample: 25% degradation (acceptable)
- 18/24 months profitable, worst month -0.68%

**PDH/PDL Best: trail 20pt, no buffer, no filter**
- Sharpe 1.66, PF 1.35, 406 trades, max DD -1.21%
- Split-sample: OOS Sharpe 2.82 (better than in-sample — very encouraging)

**Why it works:** Max 1 trade/day = ₹350 cost vs ₹1,750+ for Supertrend. Edge is in trailing stop discipline, not breakout prediction. ~₹170/day per lot (2.2 Nifty points/day).

**Anti-martingale: NOT beneficial** — 44-47% win rate doesn't produce enough streaks.

### Files
- Strategies: `nifty50-analysis/strategies/orb-breakout/`
- Code: `backtest_orb.py`, `backtest_pdhl.py`, `sweep_orb.py`, `sweep_pdhl.py`
- Results: `results/VERDICT.md`, `results/orb_sweep.csv`, `results/pdhl_sweep.csv`
- Equity curve: `results/equity_best.png`
- Trade log: `results/trade_log_best.csv`
- Old dead strategies: `strategies/odd-lot-reversal/`, `strategies/trend-continuation/`

### Data
- 24 months 1-min OHLC in `historical_nifty_data_2_years/`
- 185,446 rows, 495 trading days
- No volume, no options chain, no OI/IV/VIX

### Next steps when resuming
1. Paper trade both strategies simultaneously for 1 month
2. Need live data feed (Shoonya broker API or similar)
3. Build paper trading bot using existing backtest logic
4. Track: actual fills vs backtest assumptions, slippage reality, signal timing
5. After 1 month: compare paper results to backtest. If within 30% of backtest Sharpe, consider live with minimal capital.

### Legal/Compliance
- SEBI algo framework mandatory April 2026 — paper trading unregulated
- STT 0.15% from April 2026 (already in ₹25/lot assumption)
- Live needs: static IP + Shoonya broker

**Why:** Build confidence through rigorous backtesting before risking capital. ORB/PDH produce the first statistically robust edge found across 400+ parameter combinations.

nodes.label: project qmd wiki rag
nodes.description: qmd LIVE since 2026-05-06. Karpathy LLM-Wiki pattern. Vault indexed (87 files, 292 chunks) via junction. MCP user-scope, GPU-local models, $0 cost.
nodes.bodyMarkdown:
---
name: qmd Vault RAG (Karpathy LLM-Wiki pattern)
description: qmd by Tobi Lütke installed 2026-05-06. Indexes Obsidian vault (87 files, 292 chunks) via junction. MCP auto-loads. Replaces LightRAG. GPU-local models, zero API cost.
type: project
originSessionId: ff5e12d5-8969-458c-a959-1dba58801c49
---
## Status: LIVE since 2026-05-06

Replaces LightRAG. Implements Karpathy's "LLM Wiki" pattern (April 2026 gist).

## Install

- Package: `@tobilu/qmd` v2.1.0 (npm, NOT bun — bun's Windows shim breaks on shell-script bins)
- Binary: `[redacted]\AppData\Roaming\npm\qmd.cmd`
- Models cached at `~/.cache/qmd/models/` (~970 MB total, downloaded on first use):
  - Embedding: `embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0.gguf` (333 MB)
  - Reranker: `qwen3-reranker-0.6b-q8_0.gguf` (639 MB)
  - Query expansion: `qmd-query-expansion-1.7B-gguf` (downloaded on first complex query)
- Index: `[redacted]\.cache\qmd\index.sqlite`
- GPU: Vulkan offload to RTX 5070, 22 GB free VRAM

## Vault collection

- Collection name: `vault` (single collection currently)
- Path: `[redacted]\Projects\nikunj-vault\` ← junction created via `mklink /J`
- Real path: `[redacted]\Projects\Nikunj's Vault\`
- **Why junction**: qmd's glob library breaks on the apostrophe in `Nikunj's Vault`. Junction is a transparent fix; vault writes still go to the original folder.
- Stats: 87 markdown files, 292 chunks embedded, 32-second initial embed

## MCP server

- Registered via: `claude mcp add-json -s user qmd '{"type":"stdio","command":"cmd","args":["/c","qmd","mcp"],"env":{}}'`
- Scope: USER (available in all projects, not project-local)
- Stdio handshake: ~0.8s, advertises 87 docs in `vault` collection
- Tools: `query`, `get`, `multi_get`, `status`
- Note: `claude mcp list` may report "Failed to connect" on Windows due to short health-check timeout, but the server actually works fine when invoked through MCP protocol

## CLI cheat sheet

```bash
qmd query "natural language question"    # hybrid search (lex + vec + rerank), best UX
qmd search "exact phrase"                 # BM25 only, fast
qmd vsearch "conceptual question"         # vector only
qmd update                                # re-index after vault changes (incremental)
qmd embed                                 # regenerate embeddings if needed
qmd status                                # index health, model paths, GPU info
qmd collection list                       # show collections
qmd ls vault                              # list indexed files
qmd get path/to/note.md                   # retrieve single doc
qmd get path/to/note.md:42 -l 20          # retrieve doc with line offset
```

## Junction creation (in case it ever breaks)

```cmd
mklink /J "[redacted]\Projects\nikunj-vault" "[redacted]\Projects\Nikunj's Vault"
```

Junction created via batch file at `Projects/sessions/2026-05-06-qmd-rag-migration/make-junction.bat`. Junctions don't need admin privileges (unlike symlinks).

## Karpathy LLM-Wiki principles

1. **Compile, don't retrieve.** Synthesize sources into structured wiki pages with `[[wikilinks]]`. Raw sources go in `research/` or session folders; understanding goes in MOCs and project pages.
2. **Wiki compounds over time.** Every session should leave the vault more interlinked.
3. **Periodic linting.** Watch for orphan notes, missing backlinks, stale frontmatter, contradictory facts.

## Re-indexing workflow

After significant vault changes (new projects, major rewrites, bulk note additions):

```bash
qmd update         # detects new/changed/removed files, re-chunks
qmd embed          # generates embeddings only for changed chunks
```

Cost: incremental, only re-processes diffs. Initial full embed was 32s for 87 files; updates are typically <5s.

## Known issues

- Apostrophe in real vault path breaks qmd globs → use junction (already in place)
- `claude mcp list` health check times out on Windows → harmless, MCP works
- Bun global install creates broken shim → install via npm instead
- First `qmd query` downloads 639 MB reranker model → expected one-time cost

## Phase 2 (deferred, for next session)

- Build a "compiler" skill that takes raw drops in a `/raw` folder and synthesizes into vault wiki pages with backlinks
- Re-process the 20 YouTube transcripts (currently sitting in LightRAG storage) through the compiler so they live as proper vault pages
- Add a vault-linting pass that finds orphan notes and missing backlinks

## How to apply

When user asks about RAG, knowledge search, "where did I write about X", or wants to query their second brain — use qmd. Either via the MCP `query` tool or via Bash `qmd query "..."`. After making vault edits, run `qmd update` to keep the index current. When ingesting new sources, follow Karpathy's compile-don't-retrieve principle: synthesize into vault pages, don't just dump.

nodes.bodyMarkdown:
---
name: wiki-compiler skill
description: Phase 2 of qmd LLM-Wiki migration. Compiles raw sources (md/txt/url) into structured wiki pages (sources/entities/concepts) under Nikunj's Vault/wiki/. Built and tested 2026-05-07; manual install required at [redacted]
type: project
originSessionId: ff5e12d5-8969-458c-a959-1dba58801c49
---
## Status: INSTALLED + AVAILABLE 2026-05-07 (46 unit tests + 12 smoke green; first-run validation on real vault PENDING for next session)

Skill bundle at `Projects/_templates/skills/wiki-compiler/`. Junction-installed at `[redacted]` via `Projects/sessions/2026-05-07-wiki-compiler/install-skill.bat` (mklink /J). Skill auto-loads in every Claude Code session and appears in the available-skills list. Activate via `/compile-wiki` or natural language ("compile", "ingest", "process raw", "synthesize this transcript").

## First-run validation steps (do in next fresh session)

1. Drop a real source into the vault raw folder:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p "[redacted]/Projects/Nikunj's Vault/raw"
   cp [redacted]/Projects/lightrag-local/transcripts/<one>.txt "[redacted]/Projects/Nikunj's Vault/raw/"
   ```
2. In Claude Code, type `/compile-wiki` or say "compile the new transcript in raw"
3. Verify:
   - New pages appear in `wiki/sources/`, `wiki/entities/`, `wiki/concepts/`
   - Pages have valid YAML frontmatter (Obsidian Dataview reads them)
   - `qmd query "<entity name>"` returns the new page
   - AUTO-LINKS block appears on at least one Project page or MOC mentioning a touched entity/concept
   - Manifest entry has `compile_status: "success"`
4. After validation, edit this file: change status to `VALIDATED 2026-05-07 on <source-name>` and commit `_templates/` branch `wiki-compiler`.

## Architecture

- `SKILL.md` (397 lines): synthesis judgment (entity/concept extraction with strict JSON schema, page body generation, merge decisions, ownership contract enforcement)
- 5 Python scripts in `scripts/`:
  - `hash_source.py`: SHA-256 with line-ending normalization, trailing-whitespace strip, binary rejection
  - `manifest.py`: atomic CRUD + PID-aware locking via `psutil.pid_exists` + 30-min mtime staleness fallback + orphan-temp warning on read
  - `scan_raw.py`: walk raw/, hash each file, classify status (new/changed/unchanged/malformed)
  - `fetch_url.py`: full SSRF guards via Python `ipaddress` stdlib (loopback/private/link-local/reserved/multicast/unspecified) + IPv4-mapped IPv6 unwrap + scheme allow-list (http/https) + .url file format validation
  - `reindex.py`: thin wrapper around `qmd update`
- Stage-then-commit execution: parallel subagents stage to `wiki/.staging/<run-id>/<hash>/`; main session commits serially with single-threaded auto-link reconcile
- State: `wiki/.manifest.json` keyed by SHA-256 content hash

## Vault folder convention

```
Nikunj's Vault/
|-- raw/                   <- user drops sources here
|-- wiki/
|   |-- .manifest.json
|   |-- .staging/<run-id>/<hash>/
|   |-- _conflicts/
|   |-- sources/
|   |-- entities/
|   `-- concepts/
|-- Projects/              <- human-owned, compiler appends to <!-- AUTO-LINKS:START --> block only
|-- Systems/               <- human-owned, auto-link only
|-- MOC - *.md             <- human-owned, auto-link only
`-- ...
```

## Trigger phrases

- `/compile-wiki`
- "compile", "ingest", "process raw", "synthesize this transcript", "build wiki from"

## Files in raw/

- `.md` and `.txt`: read directly
- `.url`: single-line file with URL, fetched via crawl4ai with SSRF guards

## Manual install

```bash
# Copy or junction _templates/skills/wiki-compiler/ to [redacted]
cp -r [redacted]/Projects/_templates/skills/wiki-compiler [redacted]/.claude/skills/
# OR
cmd //c "mklink /J [redacted]\.claude\skills\wiki-compiler [redacted]\Projects\_templates\skills\wiki-compiler"

# New dependency
pip install psutil
```

Restart Claude Code so the skill loads.

## Spec, plan, build artifacts

- Spec: `Projects/sessions/2026-05-07-wiki-compiler/wiki-compiler-design.md` (rev3, approved by reviewer subagent after 3 iterations)
- Plan: `Projects/sessions/2026-05-07-wiki-compiler/wiki-compiler-plan.md` (3078 lines, 18 tasks, plan-reviewer-approved after fixing 2 placeholder blockers)
- Code: `Projects/_templates/skills/wiki-compiler/` (git branch `wiki-compiler`)
- Tests: 46 unit tests in `tests/test_*.py`, 12 smoke assertions in `tests/smoke.sh`, all green

## Phase 3 deferred

- Re-ingest of 20 YouTube transcripts in `Projects/lightrag-local/transcripts/`
- PDF/image OCR
- Synthesis essays (cross-cutting `wiki/synthesis/`)
- Vault-wide lint pass (orphans, missing backlinks, stale auto-link entries)
- Per-fetch unit tests with proper crawl4ai mocking
- Merge-path unit tests (currently no coverage of `action: "update"` flow)

## How to apply

When user mentions wiki, compiler, ingest, or wants to turn sources into structured knowledge: this skill. After build is validated by first run on real vault content, this is the canonical pattern for Karpathy LLM-Wiki on the user's vault. Phase 1 (qmd retrieval) shipped 2026-05-06; Phase 2 (compiler) built 2026-05-07.

Take-home technical challenge for AMZ Prep (logistics company). Role: AI Engineering Manager. Sent by Sarah, evaluated by Blair (founder). Deadline: April 8, 2026 5:42 PM EDT.

## Current Status: SUBMITTED — Loom recorded, GitHub repo shared with AMZ Prep (April 6, 2026)

All 7 phases built, tested, audited by Gemini, audit fixes applied, all TEST_PLAN.md cases pass.

## Key Files
- **Project root:** `[redacted]\Projects\amzprep-bidding-freight-agent\`
- **CLAUDE.md** — the full project brief (authoritative spec)
- **instructions/** — BUILD_STEPS.md, PROMPTS.md, TEST_PLAN.md (internal instructions, not deliverables)

## What Was Built
- State machine: INTAKE → OUT_TO_CARRIERS → FIRST_ROUND_RECEIVED → REBID_ROUND → QUOTE_SENT → AWAITING_APPROVAL → APPROVED/LOST
- 6 mock carriers (4 messy text, 2 clean JSON), 4 mock customers with different markups and carrier prefs
- Claude API (claude-sonnet-4-6) used at 4 points: intake parsing, rate normalization, decision explanation, quote summary
- Template fallbacks for all 4 Claude uses — pipeline never crashes if LLM down (tested with broken API key)
- SHA-256 fingerprint dedup (idempotency)
- Rebid round with time-bound enforcement (late responses rejected)
- Tiebreaker: transit → response time → alphabetical (dynamic log messages)
- Customer-specific markup (WOOF_PET 12%, SILVERTS 8%, TECHGEAR 15%, FRESHBOX 5%)
- Simulated email communications at every touchpoint (acknowledgement, carrier requests, rebid notifications, quote, approve/reject)
- Analytics endpoint: /api/analytics with win rate by carrier, conversion rate, avg markup, avg margin, lost reasons
- Try/catch ERROR state on every orchestrator step
- /health endpoint
- Demo page with scenario dropdowns + Raw Email examples + timer display + MVP notes everywhere
- Walkthrough presentation (10 slides) ordered per Sarah's ask

## Audit Fixes Applied (from Gemini audit)
- Port aligned to 4000 everywhere (server.js, README.md, .env.example)
- package.json: main=server.js, start script, description, author
- /health endpoint added
- Orchestrator try/catch with ERROR state
- Tiebreaker log message now dynamic (not hardcoded)

## Completed
- Git repo: https://github.com/nikunjsurya/amzprep-bidding-freight-agent
- Loom video recorded and submitted

- PandaDoc integration
- Analytics dashboard UI (basic /api/analytics endpoint exists)

## Design Decisions (user's voice)
- Hard filter for carrier preferences (not a scoring threshold) — per CLAUDE.md spec
- AI at 4 points only, everything else deterministic
- Flag ambiguity, don't guess (requires_manual_review)
- Template fallbacks so pipeline never depends on LLM
- Audit trail as foundation for production analytics
- communications.js not in original spec but justified by Blair's scope doc ("automates carrier and customer communications")

## Blair's Evaluation Criteria (all 10 addressed)
1. Architecture clarity — ARCHITECTURE.md + walkthrough
2. State machine — orchestrator with enforced state transitions
3. Idempotency — SHA-256 fingerprint
4. Rate parsing — Claude + fallbacks
5. Time-bound — late responses rejected
6. Margin flexibility — per-customer config
7. Error handling — try/catch + ERROR state + fallbacks
8. Data model — FreightQuote with all fields
9. Audit trail — every transition logged
10. Scalability — config-driven carrier list

**Why this exists**: Surya is a night-shift worker (Tue-Sat 10pm-6:30am EST) preparing for the TEF Canada French exam (2026-08-01) with a work-permit deadline (2026-09-13). Traditional habit apps assume midnight day-reset and fixed schedules — this app has configurable reset hour (default 18:00 EST) and fully editable weekly schedule + date overrides.

**Stack**: Next 16.2.3 · React 19 · Tailwind v4 (CSS-first `@theme`) · shadcn/ui · motion (rebranded Framer) · Zustand · @supabase/ssr · date-fns-tz · vitest · Supabase cloud (project ref `vjbgysqnpenkaywjlgrh`).

**Milestone 0 — Foundation (shipped 2026-04-12)**:
- Scaffold, design tokens (palette #0a0e1a + glow utilities), Rajdhani/Orbitron via next/font
- `src/lib/time/getEffectiveDate.ts` — DST-safe day reset, 9/9 tests pass
- 11-table Postgres schema + RLS at `supabase/migrations/20260412000000_init.sql`
- Auth: **OTP code flow** (not magic link — magic links failed due to Gmail prefetch + PKCE cookie cross-session issues)
- Bootstrap helper creates player + settings + 7 schedule rows idempotently on first dashboard visit
- Protected `(app)` layout with bottom nav, dashboard shell, countdown tiles
- PWA manifest + generated icons via Next 16 `icon.tsx`/`apple-icon.tsx`

**Milestone 1 — Dashboard MVP (shipped 2026-04-12)**:
- Progression math at `src/lib/game/progression.ts` — XP/level/rank with cascading levelups, reverse-XP via total-lifetime reconstruction (undo de-levels correctly). 10 tests.
- Schedule engine at `src/lib/schedule/resolve.ts` — merges weekly template + date overrides with partial-override support. 5 tests.
- 6 seed habits on bootstrap: Hydration Protocol, Duolingo Drill, Harman French Course, Memrise Sweep, Recovery Cycle, AI Project Session. Difficulty E-B, XP 10-25, stat rewards in JSONB.
- Server actions at `src/app/(app)/actions.ts` — `completeHabit` (XP multiplier, level-up, rank-up detection, per-habit streak, combo streak, xp_history breadcrumb) and `undoHabit` (clean reverse math).
- Dashboard at `src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx` — quest list with difficulty badges, streak flames, tap-to-DO, optimistic UI, inline undo, XP bar animation, combo counter, today's schedule row.
- Celebration modal at `src/components/quests/quest-board.tsx` — level-up, rank-up (gold glow), all-clear day messages.

**Key Supabase settings that had to be right**:
- `mailer_autoconfirm: true` (Confirm email = OFF)
- OTP length is **8 digits** (not 6) in this project — login UI accepts 6-10
- Email templates "Confirm sign up" + "Magic link" must contain `{{ .Token }}`

**Next 16 gotchas encountered**:
- `cookies()` is async — must `await`
- `middleware.ts` renamed to `proxy.ts`, export `proxy()` not `middleware()`
- Tailwind v4 is CSS-first — no `tailwind.config.ts`, put tokens in `globals.css @theme inline`

**Not yet built**:
- Stats radar chart (habit data → stat growth over time)
- Boss quests UI (dramatic milestones)
- Journal (daily reflection)
- Analytics (XP over time, completion heatmap)
- Schedule editor UI (currently only DB-seeded)

---
name: Shorts Factory — Remotion AI Video Pipeline
description: Data-driven YouTube Shorts generator at Projects/shorts-factory. 2026-05-09 update — T9 Notebook Editorial (light) + T15 Subject Skin engine (dark, 4 brand skin packs) + auto-router shipped. Catalog collapsed from 12 → 6 templates.
type: project
originSessionId: 84389824-4230-42f8-9c3f-2d098cd373bc
---
## Status (2026-05-09)

**Phase:** T9 + T15 shipped end-to-end with Hinglish narrations. Channel-decode finding rewrote the catalog: DIY Smart Code is not a multi-template channel, it's 1 light editorial lane + 1 dark "Subject Skin" engine with per-brand skin packs. Catalog collapsed accordingly.
**Project:** `[redacted]\Projects\shorts-factory\`

## Catalog (6 templates total, post-collapse)

| # | Template | Status | Lane |
|---|---|---|---|
| T1 | Mythos Launch | built (existing) | Hindi flagship launches |
| T2 | Restricted/Redacted | built (existing) | Gated/leaked releases |
| T4 | Terminal/Diagnostic | built (existing) | Open-source, dev-tool drops |
| **T9** | **Notebook Editorial** | **NEW 2026-05-09** | Opinion / hot-take / methodology |
| **T15** | **Subject Skin engine + 4 skin packs** | **NEW 2026-05-09** | Brand-specific news (Anthropic / Google / Vercel / Qwen) |

T5/T8 dropped (Notebook covers Magazine; Subject Skin covers Product Shot). T3/T6/T7/T11/T12 deferred to lowest priority — Subject Skin's two-stat pair, kicker pill, and bar-chart card cover their use cases.

## What's built (2026-05-09 session)

### T9 Notebook Editorial — light cream lane
**Folder:** `src/notebook/`
- `types.ts` — beat-discriminated schema (5 beat types: hook / slashList / statBox / serifVs / cta) + canonical palette
- `components/` — DoodleLayer (10 hand-drawn motifs), NotebookCanvas, BrandStrip, BottomChip, KickerLabel, SerifTitle (delayed crimson italic emphasis), FileBadge, PillTag, HinglishCaptionLight (cream pill backdrop)
- `scenes/` — NotebookHookScene, SlashListScene, StatBoxScene, SerifVersusScene, TerracottaCTAScene
- `NotebookShort.tsx` — beat-driven sequencer
- `topics/markdown-dying.ts` — Hinglish "Markdown is dying" thesis test

### T15 Subject Skin — dark, brand-adaptive engine
**Folder:** `src/subject/`
- `types.ts` — discriminated-union scene schema (hook / bigStat / numberedList / embed / quote / cta), engine palette, color cycle
- `skins/` — `_types.ts` (SkinSpec interface), `index.ts` (registry), `anthropic.ts`, `google.ts`, `vercel.ts`, `qwen.ts`
- `components/` — SubjectCanvas, WatermarkLayer (4 sprite types: constellation / confetti dots / red glow / purple beam), BrandStrip (per-letter coloring for Google), FooterChip (3 footer variants), StatusPill, NumberedCard, BigStatCard, ProgressBar5Step (Google's persistent pipeline bar)
- `scenes/` — HookScene, BigStatScene, NumberedListScene, EmbedScene, QuoteScene, CtaScene
- `SubjectSkinShort.tsx` — engine sequencer (renders progress bar above scenes if skin spec has one)
- `topics/` — opus-4-7-launch, google-gemini-leak, vercel-breach, qwen-3-5-weights

### Auto-router
- `src/router.ts` — keyword → template+skin selector. Subject keywords (anthropic/claude/google/gemini/vercel/qwen) pick the skin; opinion/manifesto signals route to Notebook. 6 test cases included.

## Renders shipped (5 MP4s, all in `out/`)

| File | Composition ID | Skin | Duration |
|---|---|---|---|
| notebook-markdown-dying.mp4 | NotebookMarkdownDying | T9 light | ~30s |
| subject-anthropic-opus-4-7.mp4 | SubjectAnthropicOpus47 | T15a coral | ~28s |
| subject-google-gemini-leak.mp4 | SubjectGoogleGeminiLeak | T15b multicolor + 5-step bar | ~36s |
| subject-vercel-breach.mp4 | SubjectVercelBreach | T15c red | ~32s |
| subject-qwen-weights.mp4 | SubjectQwenWeights | T15d violet | ~31s |

T9 also re-rendered as `notebook-markdown-dying-v11.mp4` with v1.1 polish (10 doodle motifs incl. squiggles + checkmark, hook size 132, statValue size 220).

## How to apply
1. **New subject (e.g. OpenAI)**: write `src/subject/skins/openai.ts` (SkinSpec), register in `index.ts`, optionally drop wordmark SVG in `public/skins/openai/`. Author topic config + narration. Register Composition. Render. ~30 min for a new skin.
2. **New T9 thesis topic**: write `src/notebook/topics/<topic>.ts` (NotebookShortProps with beats), generate narration via `Projects/sessions/2026-05-09-shorts-template-reverse/generate-t9-narration.mjs` adapted, register Composition.
3. **Auto-route a topic**: import `routeTemplate` from `src/router.ts`, pass `{category, keywords}`, get `{template, skin?, reason}`.

## Flags / lessons
- **Catalog collapse insight:** the "8 dark templates" plan in PROPOSAL.md was wrong. DIY uses 1 dark engine with palette/watermark/wordmark swap. Mirror that — never branch the engine, just add skin specs.
- **Subject wordmarks** are real brand marks (Anthropic/Google/Vercel/Qwen). DIY uses them openly as editorial. Trademark caveat: keep as `public/skins/<name>/wordmark.svg` static assets when going production.
- **Hinglish narration overruns** are normal; the generator pads short, runs raw on overruns. Always rebalance frame durations after first narration pass.
- **Devanagari + English baseline alignment** in mixed-script kickers needs a small font-feature-settings fix (Hindi sits a few px below English when same font-size).
- **The 5-step progress bar** must be rendered OUTSIDE the Sequence wrapper so it persists across scenes. Pass cumulative scene boundaries; it computes active step from useCurrentFrame().

## Session archives
- `Projects/sessions/2026-05-09-shorts-template-reverse/` — full session: PROPOSAL.md (initial T9 decode), CHANNEL_RESEARCH.md (~590 lines, full DIY decode), CHANNEL_INVENTORY.json, channel/ (13 reference MP4s + frames + 78 thumbs + 13 firstframes), STATUS.md (wrap-up), 3 narration generators, audio-* per-topic clip libraries, t9-frames/ + t15-*-frames/ verification gallery
- `Projects/sessions/2026-04-17-shorts-factory-autonomous-spike/` — earlier spike: 3 templates (Mythos/Restricted/Terminal), Hinglish topic configs

nodes.bodyMarkdown:
---
name: Shorts Factory — Adaptive Scene Engine V1
description: Beat-based adaptive Remotion shorts system. 11 scene components, brand-swappable header+watermark, 36 ElevenLabs-generated SFX, word-timestamp narration. V5 shipped 2026-04-18 (Claude Code v2.1.111 test).
type: project
originSessionId: 236c9f73-46f3-4562-948b-93172b84212a
---
## Status (2026-04-18)

**Phase:** V5 test Short rendered — Hindi/Hinglish replica of DIY Smart Code's v2.1.111 Short. Adaptive engine validated end-to-end. Ready for (a) Nikunj-voice swap when user verifies ElevenLabs clone, (b) Devanagari-script experiment for Hindi fluency, (c) production roll-out to real news ingestion pipeline.

**Project folder:** `[redacted]\Projects\shorts-factory\` (same repo as fixed-template shorts-factory; adaptive system lives alongside under `src/adaptive/`).

## Architecture

```
Hindi AI news event
  → script JSON (beats[] with {id, intent, text, scene, props, sfxHints})
  → narration generator (ElevenLabs Rudra + word timestamps)
  → narration-<id>.mp3 + captions-<id>.json {words[], beats[], duration}
  → AdaptiveShort composition (Remotion)
      → BrandHeader + BrandWatermark + ChannelWatermark (always on)
      → SfxLayer (beat-aware, density ~8 events/90s)
      → Scene router: maps captions.beats to Sequence(from=startFrame, dur=endFrame-startFrame)
      → SceneFade wrapper on each scene (7-frame in, 5-frame out — no whooshes)
      → HinglishCaption (burned-in, suppressed during text-dominant scenes)
  → mp4 + publish
```

## Scene component library (src/adaptive/scenes/)

Eleven scenes, each reads standardized `SceneProps` from `types.ts`:
- **VersionReveal** — big version number bloom (coral/cyan/brand accent)
- **CommandPills** — 1-3 glowing slash-command pills
- **CounterPain** — animated counter + growing item list with count badges
- **SkillLabel** — "SKILL ONE / /command" centered header
- **MockTerminal** — traffic-light window, prompt, status, log block, coral scan line sweep, green ✓ success row
- **MockEditor** — traffic-light window, syntax-highlighted JSON, per-line appear with green checks
- **SkillDone** — green pill "Done" + tease line
- **ProgressBars** — 5 agent progress rows, color-per-row, fill animation
- **SeverityCards** — finding cards with HIGH/MED/LOW badges + confidence + file:line
- **BrollClip** — full-bleed video b-roll, Ken Burns zoom, caption overlay, gradient bottom
- **CTAOutro** — final glowing box with brand + pills + CTA + source URL

## Brand system (src/adaptive/brand.ts)

Ten brand themes: anthropic, openai, gemini, google, meta, xai, deepseek, mistral, huggingface, github + generic. Each has bg color, accent palette, watermark pattern. BrandHeader swaps glyph + display name. BrandWatermark swaps background texture (Anthropic molecules, OpenAI hexagons, Gemini gradient, etc.). Swap `props.brand` = full visual re-skin, same scene structure.

## SFX pack

36 sounds in `public/sfx/` across 6 categories (typing, ui, transitions, states, data, ambient). All generated via **ElevenLabs Sound Generation API** with "subtle/soft/quiet/background/gentle" prompts so they sit under voice.

**V5 volume ladder** (measured against DIY Smart Code reference):
- Hints: 0.17
- CounterPain whoosh: 0.13
- MockTerminal success: 0.19
- MockEditor confirm: 0.17
- ProgressBars complete: 0.19
- SeverityCards ping (×n): 0.22
- SkillDone ding: 0.26
- CTAOutro fanfare: 0.26

Total ~8-10 events per 90s video (matches DIY's measured density).

## Narration pipeline

`sessions/2026-04-18-adaptive-shorts-v1/scripts/generate-narration.mjs`:
- Input: beats[] script JSON
- Output: mp3 (concatenated, -16 LUFS) + captions JSON with word + beat timestamps
- Voice: Rudra (`vIdhHAZdn1bGjKe1dFw8`) at `eleven_multilingual_v2`
- Settings: stability 0.50 / similarity 0.80 / style 0.15 / speed 1.00 (slower, more natural cadence)
- When Nikunj Voice verified: swap VOICE_ID constant to `bn9GLx3safIIqqZj1ywg`

## Test render (V5, 2026-04-18)

- Topic: Claude Code v2.1.111 (/less-permission-prompts + /ultrareview)
- File: `shorts-factory/out/adaptive-claude-v2111-v5.mp4` (~8.6 MB, ~94s)
- Also in: `sessions/2026-04-18-adaptive-shorts-v1/out/`
- Directly comparable to DIY Smart Code's `https://youtube.com/shorts/RQ3VaSD5CCM`

## Version history

- V1 (89.7s): baseline adaptive engine, all scenes, loud SFX
- V2: -50% SFX vol, MockTerminal matched to DIY bg, scene fades added, captions suppressed on text-dominant scenes
- V3: +40% SFX vol, coral scan line, green success row added to MockTerminal, bumped by Gemini-specified dB targets
- V4: ~8 SFX events total (was 55), Rudra speed 1.05→1.00, style 0.30→0.15 — matches DIY's measured density
- V5: final SFX volume bump +20% for presence (user feedback: "more alive, soothing")

## How to apply

1. **New topic:** copy `claude-code-v2111.script.json`, edit beats[]. Keep intent keys matching scene names.
2. **New brand:** add entry to `src/adaptive/brand.ts` + glyph SVG in `BrandHeader.tsx`. Watermark pattern in `BrandWatermark.tsx`.
3. **Render:** `cd shorts-factory && npx remotion render AdaptiveClaudeV2111 out/<name>.mp4 --codec h264 --crf 20`
4. **New SFX:** add to `generate-sfx.mjs` PACK array + rerun.

## Known gaps

- **Rudra Hindi pacing feels slightly off** on Roman-script Hinglish — see feedback memory for 4 fix options (Devanagari script is top pick).
- **Ingestion pipeline not built yet** — manual topic input for now. Phase 2: n8n polls Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/etc blogs + curates + auto-scripts.
- **Auto-publish not wired** — manual YouTube upload for now. Phase 2: YouTube Data API v3 via n8n.
- **B-roll fetcher is 1-off** — manual yt-dlp + ffmpeg 9:16 conversion. Phase 2: automate per-topic.

## Content pipeline direction (confirmed 2026-04-18)

**Long-form first, Shorts as byproduct.** Final pipeline:
1. Master the Shorts format (current phase) — get visuals/audio/voice dialed.
2. Build 8–10 min long-form videos composed of multiple Short-style segments merged.
3. Extract Shorts from the long-form as cuts (not the other way around).

**Why this matters:** one narration run can fund a long-form + 5–8 derived Shorts (no extra ElevenLabs spend for the Shorts since audio is reused).

**Budget math at 10-min scale (Pro tier, 500k chars/month reset):**
- 10-min video ≈ 9,500 chars → ~52 videos/month ceiling
- Shorts cut from those = 0 additional audio cost
- Plenty of headroom even with 2x narration passes per video

**V7+ architecture needs (don't build until Shorts locked):**
- Chapter layer above beats (5–8 chapters × 60–120s each; each = current Short script)
- ChapterTransition scene with chapter number + title + soft reset
- Per-chapter progress bar with a chapter ticker underneath
- Brand-per-chapter (10-min weekly recap could mix Anthropic + OpenAI + Gemini chapters)
- Shorts extraction script: input long-form mp4 + captions JSON → cut 60–90s chunks on beat boundaries, re-render with Short-sized progress bar + outro
- AdaptiveShort already supports most of this — just needs a parent composition that sequences multiple sub-scripts each with own beats[]

## V6 SHIPPED 2026-04-18

**Topic:** OpenAI Codex "superapp" update (computer use + memory + SSH + 90 plugins, announced 2026-04-17). Picked over Claude Opus 4.7 and Gemini 3 Deep Think for brand variety (V5 was Claude) and because it perfectly fits the new ComparisonCards component (Codex vs Claude Code rivalry).

**Render:** `shorts-factory/out/adaptive-v6-openai-codex.mp4` → `sessions/2026-04-18-adaptive-shorts-v1/out/adaptive-v6-openai-codex.mp4` (5.8 MB, 68.6s, 9 beats, 153 words, Rudra).

**Tier 1 built:**
- T1.1 cold-open-stinger.mp3 (ElevenLabs Sound Gen, B3 layered stab, plays at frame 0 via SfxLayer v=0.35)
- T1.2 MockTweetCard scene (X dark-mode card, verified blue check SVG, engagement bar, embedType=docs|image|none)
- T1.3 MockBrowserFrame component (traffic lights + address bar + screenshot slot + chip tabs)
- T1.4 DocsPreviewCard sub-component (section/headline/subtext/sourceDomain)
- T1.5 `remotion-dev/skills` installed (skills-lock.json, .claude/skills/remotion-best-practices/)

**Tier 2 built:**
- T2.1 CTAOutro 4 variants via outroStyle prop: subscribe | follow | pick | comment | version (legacy default)
- T2.2 SlideInBadge component (orange pill, enterFrom top-left|top-right|bottom, spring entry)
- T2.3 ComparisonHeader (persistent logo-vs-logo row, swaps BrandHeader when headerStyle="comparison") + ComparisonCards scene (side-by-side product cards, winner overlay)
- T2.4 Top-edge orange ProgressBar at root of AdaptiveShort (4px, always visible, fills L→R across full duration)

**New scenes added to SCENE_MAP:** MockTweetCard, ComparisonCards, BrowserReveal, BadgeOverlay.

**New types:** `headerStyle`, `comparisonHeader` on AdaptiveShort schema; scene-specific data carried via passthrough (`props.tweet`, `props.comparison`, `props.browser`, `props.badge`).

**Known issues to fix in V6.1:**
- BadgeOverlay badge exits too early (holdFrames=90 is short for 5s+ beats) — make hold dynamic to beat duration.
- ComparisonCards "winner" overlay overlaps one feature row — relocate below cards or to bottom-center.
- VersionReveal with long text ("SUPERAPP") renders too dim vs version strings ("v2.1.111") — add a large-text mode.
- Consider adding BadgeOverlay + CommandPills to HinglishCaption suppress list for scenes that already display prominent text.

**Session artifacts:** `sessions/2026-04-18-adaptive-shorts-v1/scripts/v6-openai-codex.json`, `generate-cold-open-sfx.mjs`, `out/v6-frames/` (10 JPEGs sampled at 0.5/6/12/20/28/36/44/52/60/66s).

## V6 spec (historical — now shipped)

**Canonical V6 execution spec:** `[redacted]\Projects\sessions\2026-04-18-adaptive-shorts-v1\V6-EXECUTION-SPEC.md`

**Headline V6 additions (from deep research on 4 DIY shorts):**
1. Cold-open stinger at frame 0 — DIY has a byte-identical ~350ms B3-pitch layered stab across all his shorts. Extract at `common-entry-sfx.wav` in session folder for reference; V6 regenerates a brand-equivalent via ElevenLabs Sound Generation API.
2. MockTweetCard scene — DIY's primary proof device (used with @bcherny, @claudeai). Includes nested embed slot for DocsPreviewCard or product screenshot.
3. MockBrowserFrame component — traffic lights + URL bar + embedded screenshot. Used for product/landing-page reveals.
4. 4 outro variants — subscribe / follow-pill / pick-below / comment-below. DIY rotates per short to avoid habituation.
5. SlideInBadge + ComparisonHeader + ComparisonCards for versus-style shorts.
6. Install `remotion-dev/skills` — Anthropic's official 28+ rule file pack teaches Claude Code the Remotion idioms.
7. Whisper word-level captions — optional T6 upgrade over current ElevenLabs timestamp chunks.

**V6 scope locked as Tier 1 + Tier 2** (see spec). Tier 3 (AI review loop, niche YAML profiles, LogoTrustGrid) deferred further.

**To resume:** user will paste the resume-V6 prompt in a new session. Follow the spec file exactly.

## V6.1 SHIPPED 2026-04-19

Three bug fixes from V6 known-issues list, plus caption suppress expansion. Validated on both OpenAI Codex re-render and fresh Gemini 3 Deep Think stress-test topic.

**Fixes:**
- **SlideInBadge dynamic hold** (`src/adaptive/components/SlideInBadge.tsx`): exit timing now driven by beat duration. `holdFrames` is optional; when omitted, badge exits in last ~12 frames of the beat. Old default of 90 frames caused badge to disappear at ~3.7s regardless of beat length (bad for 7-13s beats).
- **VersionReveal auto-sizing** (`src/adaptive/scenes/VersionReveal.tsx`): font size steps down by text length — 200pt (≤8 chars), 160pt (9-11), 130pt (12-14), 110pt (15+). Letter spacing also relaxes for longer strings. Prevents "SUPERAPP" / "DEEP THINK" from clipping edges or looking dim.
- **ComparisonCards winner banner** (`src/adaptive/scenes/ComparisonCards.tsx`): `WinnerRibbon` replaced with `WinnerBanner` rendered below both cards (not above one card). Includes directional arrow (◀ for winner="left", ▶ for "right") so side is unambiguous.
- **Caption suppress list** (`src/adaptive/AdaptiveShort.tsx`): added `BadgeOverlay` and `CommandPills` to the no-caption scene list. Both already display prominent on-screen text; adding them to the suppress list avoids visual stacking.

**Renders:**
- `out/adaptive-v6-1-openai-codex.mp4` — same V6 script, now with V6.1 fixes (5.6 MB, 68.6s)
- `out/adaptive-v6-1-gemini-deepthink.mp4` — fresh stress-test topic, Gemini brand, winner=right, 10-char version string, 13.6s BadgeOverlay beat (4.8 MB, 63.6s)
- Script: `src/adaptive-configs/gemini-deepthink-v6-1.json` (also in `sessions/2026-04-18-adaptive-shorts-v1/scripts/`)
- Narration: `public/narration-gemini-deepthink-v6-1.mp3` + captions

**Known issue noted during V6.1 testing (not fixed):**
- `HinglishCaption` 6-word chunks overflow the 960px-wide container on long Devanagari/Hinglish phrases (e.g., "Background mein multiple agents parallel chalate"). Visible in MockTerminal beat of V6 render. Fix for V6.2: hard-cap chunk at 4 words or add auto-font-size based on chunk char count.

## V6.2 SHIPPED 2026-04-19

**Fix:** HinglishCaption chunk overflow resolved. `chunkWords` now caps at `target + 1` words (4 for default target=3) AND 38 characters, whichever hits first. Previous `HARD_MAX = target * 2` (6 words) let long Hinglish/Devanagari phrases exceed the 960px container and clip off the right edge.

**File:** `src/adaptive/components/HinglishCaption.tsx`

**Re-render:** `out/adaptive-v6-2-openai-codex.mp4` — same V6 script with V6.2 cap. MockTerminal beat now shows clean chunks: "sakta hai, type kar" / "sakta hai. Background mein" / "hain, tumhare kaam ko", each well inside the pill.

- P2 — features_5tabs expanded from 9 words to ~70 words (one sentence per pillar).

nodes.label: notebooklm-cleaner
nodes.description: Personal-use tool. Strips visible NotebookLM branding from exported infographics (PNG/JPG) and slide decks (PPTX).
nodes.bodyMarkdown:
# notebooklm-cleaner

Personal-use tool. Strips visible NotebookLM branding from exported infographics (PNG/JPG) and slide decks (PPTX).

## Setup

```bash
pip install python-pptx Pillow opencv-python-headless
```

## PPTX deck

```bash
# Step 1: see what is on the deck
python clean_pptx.py "deck.pptx" --list-all

# Step 2: dry run, see what the auto-detector would remove
python clean_pptx.py "deck.pptx" --dry-run

# Step 3: actually clean (asks before deleting)
python clean_pptx.py "deck.pptx"

# If the watermark is missed, tell it what to look for:
python clean_pptx.py "deck.pptx" --keyword "deep dive"

# Output: deck_clean.pptx
```

Detection looks at:
1. Shape name and alt text matching `notebooklm`, `google labs`, `made with`
2. Text content matching the same
3. Small Picture shapes positioned in any corner of master / layout slides (typical badge placement)

## Infographic image

```bash
# Default: crop 80px off the bottom (NotebookLM-style badge position)
python clean_image.py "infographic.png"

# Crop different amount
python clean_image.py "infographic.png" --bottom 120

# If the watermark is somewhere weird, give bbox in pixels
python clean_image.py "infographic.png" --bbox 900,1800,1080,1900

# Auto-locate via template match (give it one logo crop, it finds it on any image)
python clean_image.py "infographic.png" --auto --template samples/logo_crop.png

# Output: infographic_clean.png
```

## Workflow tip

For infographics, the cleanest result is usually a straight crop, not inpaint. NotebookLM puts the badge at the bottom edge, so chopping 60-100px off the bottom leaves the actual content untouched.

For slides, deleting the shape from the master is the right move. It removes it from every slide at once without leaving a hole.

## V2 Status (2026-05-13)

Major upgrade pass over the V1 (2026-05-10) state. Now demonstrably produces high-variety, topic-driven Hinglish Shorts. See [[feedback_diy_smart_code_patterns]] for what we are calibrating against.

### Sprint stack shipped
- **Sprint 1**: Stab SFX under word 1 (DIY voice-first cold open), italic-serif emphasis word with line-break-before-emphasis rule, Devanagari-aware HookScene font (Hind), `+45` tail frame end-frame hold, static bg (NO particles).
- **Sprint 2**: 2-line rolling burn-in captions (DIY-style line retire on new sentence), narrow SFX bed (chime on bigStat, click_soft per numberedList item, success_ding on cta), top-edge ProgressFill bar on SubjectCanvas.
- **Sprint 3**: 4 reveal-wrapper scenes (MockTweetCard, MockBrowserFrame, ComparisonCards, UrlCommand), SceneFadeIn wrapper on every scene, bigger BulletStatus slide entrance (-80px + skin-color borders).
- **Pronunciation fix**: `shorts-factory/scripts/pronunciation-dictionary.json` (30+ entries) applied to script_text in bridge before ElevenLabs TTS. Logged per render. Auto-extended via JSON edits.
- **Notebook parity**: BeatFadeIn wrapper, NotebookProgressFill terracotta bar, narrow SFX bed (stab + chime on statBox + ding on cta).

### Recipe router (autonomous LLM)
`apply_wf01_recipe_router.py` patches the WF01 "Build Scene Plan Prompt" node. **7 recipes** classify topic via keyword:
- **launch**: hook -> mockBrowser -> bulletStatus -> comparisonCards -> cta
- **leak**: hook -> quote -> mockTweet -> bigStat -> bulletStatus -> cta
- **benchmark**: hook -> comparisonCards -> comparisonAnnotated -> bigStat -> cta
- **hot_take**: notebook-hook -> slashList -> serifVs -> statBox -> notebook-cta  (Notebook composition)
- **framework_drop**: hook -> urlCommand -> code -> numberedList -> cta

LLM only fills content for the locked shape. Recipe + skin + composition_id + cold_open_variant emitted at top level of the prompt response so downstream nodes can read them.

### Hyperframes cold-open library (7 variants)
`Projects/sessions/2026-05-13-shorts-variety-demo/hyperframes-eval/` holds one HF project per skin family. Each is a 1.6-1.8s 1080x1920 cold-open with brand wordmark + accent vibe + stab audio.
- shorts-coldopen (anthropic): coral glow ring, ANTHROP\C scale-in
- shorts-coldopen-google: multicolor letter pop + confetti + leak stripes
- shorts-coldopen-vercel: red gradient sweep + glitch triangle + INCIDENT REPORT pill

All pass `npm run check` clean. Render time ~5-6s each. Stitched into Remotion body via FFmpeg concat filter (re-encode for stream compatibility).

### Longform engine

Test artifact: `longform_claudecode_zero_to_prod.json` (3 chapters, ~4 min output). Render time ~3 min for 3 chapters.

### Bridge persistence
- `shorts-factory/scripts/start-bridge.bat` loads `.env` and starts node
- `shorts-factory/scripts/install_bridge_task.ps1` registers scheduled task `ShortsFactoryRenderBridge` to auto-start at user login. Already installed (Ready state).
- Runtime watchdog via task settings (RestartCount=5, RestartInterval=1 min).

### Live render artifacts (Telegram delivery)
Today shipped 7 reels to Telegram bot, msgs 384-393:
- 384/385/386 (baseline variety demo, hook+list+stat+cta repeated)
- 387 (Sprint 1 only — italic serif emphasis, line-break-before-emphasis)

### Session folder
`Projects/sessions/2026-05-13-shorts-variety-demo/` holds:
- 7 reel specs (reel1-7 + reel4 Gemini leak)

### Pronunciation dictionary content
30+ entries covering AI ecosystem: Hyperframes, DeepMind, Claude Code, ChatGPT, Codestral, Anthropic, OpenAI, Alibaba, Vercel, Mistral, Mixtral, Gemini, Google, Claude, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, Llama, Meta, Skills, Qwen, Gemma, Hermes, Cursor, Lambda, GitHub, Telegram, MCP, MMLU, SWE-bench, GPT-5.5/5.4/5/4, API, SDK(s), plus Hinglish particles (ka/ki/ke -> का/की/के). Extension is one-line JSON edit + bridge restart.

### Known issues / next-session work
1. WF02 doesn't yet auto-prepend HF cold-open in the autonomous /reel path. Manual stitching via batch_render_v3.py / longform_render.py works. Wiring HF prepend into bridge `/shorts/render` (accept `cold_open_variant` param) is the autonomy-completion step.
2. The 4 reveal-wrapper scenes work but LLM prompt may need tuning to consistently produce good props (especially `mockBrowser` which needs realistic blog headlines).
3. Longform: Telegram bot API caps sendVideo at 50MB. ~10-15 min videos at 1080x1920 H.264 typically land 30-50MB. Compress harder or split if >50MB.
4. Bridge auto-restart is via scheduled task only — no health-check watchdog yet. If bridge hangs (not crashes), task won't restart it.
5. Notebook editorial track upgraded but only `hot_take` recipe routes to it. Recipe router could expand Notebook usage for `methodology` and `framework_drop` style essays.

## Pre-V2 history below



End-to-end autonomous pipeline working through Telegram. The two existing AI Reel Generator workflows (originally a CSC take-home) were upgraded in-place to drive the new T9 Notebook + T15 Subject Skin engines instead of the older AdaptiveShort pipeline.

## Trigger

Send to the Telegram bot (`@SMARTCODE_DIY` / chat_id 6272541552):

```
/reel <topic>
```

- **Long input (>80 chars)**: bypasses RSS + Claude rank, treats the topic text as the news content. Produces ONE candidate card.
- **Short keyword** (`/reel claude`, `/reel gemini`): goes through full RSS fetch + Claude rank + top-3 selection. Produces THREE candidate cards. (Less battle-tested in the upgraded form; the synthetic-candidate path was the main test path.)
- **Force regenerate**: `/reel! <topic>` skips idempotency check.

User taps Approve on a card → WF02 fires → bridge renders → MP4 lands in chat.

## Architecture (from end to end)

```
Telegram /reel
  → WF01 (mj5qMn2qpB6XK60b "AI Reel Generator — 01 Research + Approval")
    → Telegram Trigger
    → Parse Command + Authorize
    → Send Acknowledgement
    → Bypass RSS? (NEW — IF on $('Parse Command + Authorize').first().json.topic.length > 80)
       branch true:  → Build Synthetic Candidate (NEW)
       branch false: → Fetch All RSS Feeds → Normalize & Dedupe → Build Filter Prompt
                       → Claude Rank → Select Top 3
       both branches converge:
    → Build Scene Plan Prompt (REWRITTEN — emits T15 schema, picks skin via keyword regex)
    → Claude Scene Plan (httpRequest → claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929)
    → Parse Scene Plans (REWRITTEN — extracts {props, script_text})
    → Format Telegram Cards (REWRITTEN — emits chat_id/text/parse_mode/reply_markup,
                              carries props + script_text + candidate metadata forward)
    → Send Cards to Telegram
    → Prep Job File (REWRITTEN — pulls cards from $('Format Telegram Cards').all(),
                      writes {candidates: [{index, label, source, title, link, props, script_text}]})
    → Write Job File (binary writer)

User taps Approve →
  → WF01 Telegram Trigger fires again with callback_query
  → routed to "Call WF2 (Render)" via Is Callback Query?
  → WF02 (SdW3hhoLhOXFfoQQ "AI Reel Generator — 02 Render + Deliver")
    → Sub-Workflow Trigger
    → Parse Callback + Authorize → Acknowledge Click → Approved?
    → Read Job File → Load Stored Job (REWRITTEN — surfaces props + script_text)
    → Build TTS Text (REWRITTEN — picks SubjectSkinShortDynamic vs NotebookShortDynamic)
    → Bridge: Shorts Render (NEW node — POST localhost:5555/shorts/render)
    → Bridge: Read MP4 (GET /mp4/<job_id>)
    → Send Video to Telegram
```

## Bridge endpoint (NEW)

`shorts-factory/scripts/render-bridge.mjs` extended with `POST /shorts/render`:

- Body: `{job_id, composition_id, props, script_text, voice_id?, model_id?}`
- Steps: ElevenLabs TTS with-timestamps → write narration mp3 → write captions JSON in HindiCaption format → rescale durationFrames proportionally to actual narration → write content/<jobId>.json → run `node scripts/batch-render.mjs --composition=<id> content/<jobId>.json`
- Returns: `{ok, job_id, mp4_path, duration_sec, words_count, scene_count, mp4_bytes, elapsed_ms}`
- ~10s end-to-end for a 5-second short, ~30-40s for a 30-second short

Bridge runs at `localhost:5555`. Start with:
```
cd shorts-factory && ELEVENLABS_API_KEY=... node scripts/render-bridge.mjs
```

## Dynamic compositions added to Root.tsx

- `SubjectSkinShortDynamic` — uses calculateMetadata to derive duration from `props.scenes`
- `NotebookShortDynamic` — same for `props.beats`
- Both pass props via Remotion CLI `--props=<file>` (handled by batch-render.mjs)

## Skin auto-pick keyword map

In WF01 Build Scene Plan Prompt, regex match drives skin selection:
- anthropic / claude / opus / sonnet / haiku / mcp → anthropic
- google / gemini / gemma / deepmind / alphaevolve → google
- vercel / nextjs → vercel
- qwen / alibaba → qwen
- openai / gpt-5 / gpt-4 / chatgpt / o1 / o3 → openai
- mistral / mixtral / codestral → mistral
- meta / llama / facebook → meta
- fallback → anthropic

## Known issues / next-session todos

1. **Old narration/captions/content/render bridge nodes are orphaned** in WF02. They have no incoming connections (rewired around them). Visual clutter only — no functional impact. Can delete in a cleanup pass.
2. **Telegram bot token is hardcoded** in some Code nodes (Parse Command + Authorize, Load Stored Job). Should be moved to an n8n credential reference. Same risk pattern existed in the original reel generator.
3. **Short-keyword RSS path** (`/reel claude`) was upgraded structurally but not retested with the new prompt. If the Build Scene Plan Prompt fails on RSS-sourced data shape, debug by running an execution and inspecting Parse Scene Plans output.
4. **No actual publishing yet** — Approve just renders + sends to Telegram. v0.2 should add YouTube/Instagram upload on approval.
5. **Caption per-scene rebalancing** is naive (proportional scale of scene durationFrames against total audio length). Per-scene TTS calls would give exact durations but require schema change to add `narration` per scene.
6. **Bridge auto-restart** — if the Windows host reboots, the bridge stops. Add a startup task or PM2 / nssm service.
7. **Approval callbacks** for OLD pre-upgrade jobs in chat scroll will fail silently because the job file format changed. User should ignore old cards. /jobs/clear via bridge POST removes them.

## Session history

- **2026-05-09** — T9 Notebook + T15 Subject Skin engine + 7 skin packs + 11 scene types + auto-router shipped
- **2026-05-10** — autonomous pipeline upgrade: Reel Generator workflows in-place upgraded to T9/T15, /shorts/render bridge endpoint added, bypass mode added, multiple field-shape mismatches debugged and fixed end-to-end. Working from fresh /reel through to MP4-in-Telegram.

nodes.bodyMarkdown:
---
name: linkedin-visual-overhaul
description: 2026-05-16 strategy session. Excalidraw monoculture replaced with 6-lane visual system (catalog/architecture/photo/screenshot/carousel/Excalidraw-survivor). Higgsfield as primary generator. Codebase changes pending.
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 210bebeb-3044-4cbd-bbde-e3aff49e9ea5
---

## Status 2026-05-16

Strategy doc complete. Codebase changes pending. Higgsfield MCP OAuth blocker for test gen.

Cross-refs:
- Current autopilot state: [[project_ai_linkedin_autopilot]]
- Brij playbook (reverse-engineered): [[reference_brij_pandey_playbook]]
- Higgsfield setup: [[reference_higgsfield_mcp]]
- Session folder: `Projects/sessions/2026-05-16-linkedin-visual-overhaul/`

## Central finding

**Brij Pandey does NOT post Excalidraw.** Neither does Prashant Varshney. Zero hand-drawn posts in 16 viewports of their recent activity. Their signature "AI LinkedIn aesthetic" is **designed infographics styled to look hand-crafted** (consistent flat icon kit, italic serif title, cream/white canvas, branded watermark, perfect alignment).

Visual mix in Surya's actual logged-in feed (20 posts):
- Designed infographic 30% (top engagement: Prashant 16 Types of RAG, 211 reactions)
- Carousel PDF 20% (Atlassian Handbook 437)
- Real photo 20% (SAP CEO Christian Klein 7,932)
- Excalidraw hand-drawn 10% (only Brij + 1 Brij-clone, Alina TA)
- Text-only 10% (near-zero engagement)
- Screenshot 5%, Meme 5%

Surya's 18-Excalidraw-template Autopilot was solving the wrong problem — the format itself is the bottleneck.

## The 6 visual lanes

| Lane | Style | Generator | When |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Designed catalog | White canvas, gradient header, 6-16 numbered cards | Higgsfield gpt_image_2 | "N Types of X" listicles |
| 2. Architecture diagram | Cream canvas, serif title, boxes+arrows, watermark | Higgsfield gpt_image_2 (Brij-style) | One concept = one frame |
| 3. Real photo | Photographic | Higgsfield text-to-image or Soul ID | Personal moments |
| 4. Screenshot frame | Tool UI / code / chat | Local PIL composer | Tool-in-action posts |
| 5. Carousel deck | Multi-page PDF | Higgsfield per-slide + PDF assembly | Reference handbooks |
| 6. Excalidraw (survivor) | Hand-drawn doodle | Existing render | Only for `napkin_sketch` |

Default lane allocation: 40% catalog, 25% architecture, 15% photo, 10% screenshot, 7% carousel, 3% Excalidraw.

## New 12-recipe library (recipe → lane)

1. `numbered_taxonomy` → lane 1 (catalog) — "N Types of X"
2. `misconception_breaker` → lane 2 (architecture) — "Most people think X, actually Y"
3. `framework_catalog` → lane 1 — "The N frameworks shaping X"
4. `tool_landscape` → lane 1 — "X categories, N tools, the map"
5. `system_architecture` → lane 2 — "How [thing] actually works"
6. `before_after_compare` → lane 2 — "Before X vs After X"
7. `personal_milestone` → lane 3 (photo) — "New chapter / thrilled to share"
8. `industry_photo_react` → lane 3 — react to public keynote/launch
9. `tool_in_action` → lane 4 (screenshot) — "I built X, here's what it does"
10. `prompt_output` → lane 4 — "Drop this prompt into Claude"
11. `mini_handbook` → lane 5 (carousel) — "Free handbook: N-page guide"
12. `napkin_sketch` → lane 6 (Excalidraw) — informal sketch

Recipes 7-8 stay `personal_only`, only auto-rotate when real event in source feed.

## New hook bans

Add to existing list: "I went down a rabbit hole", "Here are N things I learned", "Quick thread on…", emoji-in-first-line, "Game changer/revolutionary/disruptive".

## Posting cadence change

Current: 3/week Mon/Wed/Fri 9am ET.
Recommended: 4/week Tue/Thu/Sat 9am ET + Sun 6pm ET. One catalog/week, one architecture/week, one personal-or-screenshot/week, one opportunistic news-react.

Plus daily habit: comment on 5-10 high-engagement AI posts within 30min of posting (Brij, Prashant, Allie Miller, Lior).

## Codebase changes pending

Stays: WF1-WF5 architecture, 26 RSS feeds, type A/B filtering, audience lens prompt, voice library, Telegram approval, hashtag sanitization.

Changes:
- 18 Excalidraw templates → 6 visual lanes
- 12-recipe library replaces existing 12-recipe Excalidraw library
- New `generate_visual_spec` flow: recipe → lane → Higgsfield prompt template → MCP call → save → reference
- New `lane_lock` per recipe (enforces lane not specific template)
- Render service gains `/render-higgsfield` endpoint wrapping Higgsfield MCP
- Excalidraw renderer demoted to backup for lane 6 only
- New Higgsfield prompt template library at `Projects/ai-linkedin-autopilot/visual-lanes/`

## Higgsfield MCP OAuth gotcha

MCP server registered globally (2026-05-08) but requires per-session OAuth handshake. Auth URL redirects to `localhost:42242` on laptop. If user is on remote session and can't click locally, autonomous Playwright path (using `~/.higgsfield/browser_profile/` cookies) FAILED — Clerk session doesn't auto-authenticate headless instance, bounces to sign-in.

Workaround: do the click when physically at laptop. Re-trigger `mcp__higgsfield__authenticate` to get fresh URL (URLs expire). Once tools load, all generation is pure MCP, no browser.

## How to apply

When user asks to ship the actual LinkedIn changes:
1. Read `Projects/sessions/2026-05-16-linkedin-visual-overhaul/STRATEGY.md` for full context
2. Work in `Projects/ai-linkedin-autopilot/`
3. Add `visual-lanes/` directory with one prompt template per lane (start with lane 1 from `higgsfield-prompts/lane_1_catalog.md`)
4. Modify WF1 Code node `Build Visual Spec Prompt` to route by recipe's `lane` field instead of `lockedTemplate`
5. Add `/render-higgsfield` endpoint to render-service that calls Higgsfield MCP and saves PNG locally
6. Update recipe library (current 12-recipe array in WF1 Build Visual Spec Prompt code) to new 12 recipes with `lane:` field
7. Test one recipe per lane before switching default
